title: AAR — Power Play, third segment: why the nickel never deployed (and surfacing it)
scope: This segment was driven by one operator report — "no matter what I do, the nickel never activates" — and is as much about the *debugging* as the fixes.

rationale.item: The operator had the feature merged, toggled it on (per-league box and the global Engine Settings checkbox), even lowered the eligibility thresholds — and no team ever deployed a nickel.

delegation_events.decision: Eligibility/roster fixes directed by the operator
delegation_events.quote: Fixes, all from the operator's direction:

delegation_events.decision: Record nickel appearances in the stat record, not just eligibility
delegation_events.quote: the operator clarified the real intent: when reviewing a *fielder*, know he **played** nickel, not just that he's eligible.

actions.description: Stale-config fix: ensure_applied re-applies when the stored blob's signature changes (one cheap indexed lookup; full re-apply only on actual change); regression test added
Ability-based nickel eligibility: any player whose arm AND best glove (general/infield/outfield) clear the bar, regardless of listed position
Cap dedicated DHs at 3 (jokers); 2 pinch-hit specialists became loud-bat fielders at thin corners; per-team DH 5 → 3
Jokers listed at their actual field position, not "DH" — joker is a usage role (roster_slot="joker"/game_position="J"); new leagues carry zero "DH" position rows
🪙 nickel-eligible tag on team rosters using the engine's effective thresholds + scout.to_unit, shown only when the rule is active for that team
NF recorded in a fielder's stat record: per-game game_position stamped NF (extends to SS-NF); box score shows NF; player page carries a "🪙 Nickel — 10th defender in N games" badge

risk_posture: when a user insists a shipped feature doesn't work and your tests say it does, the gap is in *what your test isn't reproducing* — environment, process lifecycle, or data shape — not in the user
Safe because every joker check keys on the role tags, never the position field.

validation_claims.claim: Stale-config: reproduced → fixed → regression test; 17 engine_config tests green; after fix the same scenario fires (104 rows / 12 games)
Eligibility/firing: default thresholds now fire reliably (≈130 rows/14 games); rule-off still writes nothing
validation_claims.check_method: Sim 14 games rule-on with default thresholds; count power-play rows

validation_claims.claim: Roster shape: per-team DH = 3 (was 5); corners better stocked; jokers at real positions; joker mechanic intact
validation_claims.check_method: Seed a league and count DH-position rows / joker role tags

validation_claims.claim: Surfacing: PP-off team shows no coin; PP-on shows 🪙 on eligible players; NF recorded and shown on box score + player page
validation_claims.check_method: Render roster and player pages with the rule on/off

validation_claims.claim: Power-play suite 31, engine_config 17 — green. (One test updated to encode the new ability-based policy)
validation_claims.check_method: Run the power-play and engine_config suites

residual_risks: These all require a deploy + refresh to take effect — the stale-config fix is the linchpin: until it ships, the Engine Settings toggle still won't reach the simulator.
section_headers_original: AAR — Power Play, third segment: why the nickel never deployed (and surfacing it)
What went wrong in the diagnosis (the honest part)
Root cause #1 — stale config (the global toggle)
Root cause #2 — eligibility starved by DH-heavy benches
unmapped_content: Branch claude/power-play-optional-rule-HmsMs. Diagnosis honesty: for several rounds the agent kept reproducing success in fresh seeded test DBs and concluding the user's league must not be opted in — 'a green repro that dodges the user's actual conditions is not evidence the user is wrong.' Root cause #1 detail: engine_config applied stored tuning once per process (_applied latch); ensure_applied() was a permanent no-op after the first call, so settings toggled after process start or saved by a different worker (the norm on fly.dev) were written to the DB but never re-read; cfg.POWER_PLAY_ENABLED stayed False. The per-league flag was always immune (read from the team row each sim) — which is why isolated tests passed and this hid so long. Root cause #2 detail: nickel could only be a bench player listed at OF or SS; O27 benches skewed to bat-only DH/joker/PH players (operator noticed seven DHs); the position gate failed, not the rating gate, which is why lowering arm/field to 0.25 didn't help. 'For the operator' closing instructions: after deploy, toggle Power Play, sim fresh games; no threshold fiddling needed.
parser_notes: No date in document (checked heading, intro, commits table); 'undated'. Note: filename says 'deploy-and-eligibility' but the heading calls it the 'third segment'; the box-score-notes AAR says it follows this one.
